items.title: Claude Lemieux, former Canadien and four-time Stanley Cup champion, dead at 60
items.summary_auto: Claude Lemieux, a four-time Stanley Cup champion and once one of the NHL's most infamous antagonists, has died at the age of 60.

items.title: Mother of newborn who was found dead has also died, Halifax police say
items.summary_auto: The 23-year-old woman was in critical condition when she arrived in hospital on Friday. A newborn baby was found dead two days later in a wooded area near Halifax.

items.title: Children's Television Awards: Super Team Canada, Odd Squad get top honours at CSAs
items.summary_auto: Odd Squad, Super Team Canada, The Unstoppable Jenny Garcia and Old Enough! shine at 2026 Canadian Screen Awards Children's Television Awards presented Thursday.

items.title: Wildfire activity low so far — but a hot and dry summer could change that, officials warn
items.summary_auto: The start to the 2026 wildfire season has been slow with the number of fires raging across the country well below average, but government officials warn that as the summer progresses there's a risk things could get much worse.

items.title: Vanilla Ice, Milli Vanilli performing at America's 250th concert as others withdraw
items.summary_auto: Americans will soon sing Happy Birthday to celebrate 250 years of independence, and the official celebrations include a lineup of one-hit wonders to help revellers belt out some '90s tunes in Washington D.C.

items.title: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer says she's not running for U.S. president in 2028
items.summary_auto: Democratic Gov. Gretchen Whitmer of Michigan put to rest speculation about a potential 2028 U.S. presidential bid, saying Thursday that she will not join what is expected to be a crowded primary field after leaving office at the end of this year.

items.title: Canada, U.S., Mexico announce Ebola travel measures for FIFA World Cup
items.summary_auto: The United States, Mexico and Canada on Thursday said they will align their respective public health travel measures for people travelling to the FIFA World Cup from parts of Africa that are at the greatest risk from Ebola as the three host countries aim to protect citizens and visitors during the weeks-long event.

items.title: John Reardon returns as star of Hudson & Rex after fan backlash
items.summary_auto: Detective Charlie Hudson is back on the case. Actor John Reardon is returning to Hudson & Rex for its ninth season, Citytv announced Tuesday.

items.title: Supreme Court won’t hear appeal of Wolastoqey title ruling
items.summary_auto: Private industrial forest land in New Brunswick will remain excluded from an ownership claim by Wolastoqey First Nations after the Supreme Court of Canada turned down an application to hear the case.

items.title: Victoire captain Poulin has 'decisions to make' about PWHL future after Walter Cup win
items.summary_auto: Montréal Victoire captain Marie-Philip Poulin will navigate the coming weeks under a cloud of uncertainty that, for now, casts doubt on her immediate future in the Professional Women's Hockey League.

items.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburb, at least 14 killed across southern Lebanon
items.summary_auto: Israel's air force struck a southern suburb of Beirut on Thursday afternoon, its military said, amid a broader escalation in southern Lebanon where Israeli troops have crossed the Litani River over the past days and warned residents to leave much of the area.

items.title: U.S. Justice Department reportedly investigating Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll
items.summary_auto: The Justice Department has launched a criminal investigation into E. Jean Carroll, the writer who accused U.S. President Donald Trump of raping her in the mid-1990s, a source familiar with the matter ​told Reuters on Wednesday, after CNN first reported the development.

items.title: B.C. premier says Alberta separatism the 'elephant in the room' during pivotal moment for Canada
items.summary_auto: Even as B.C. Premier David Eby argues that his province is delivering on helping grow a Canadian economy battered by U.S. trade attacks, he said the "omnipresent" issue of a referendum on Alberta separatism has made the situation more delicate.

items.title: CFL signs 6-year broadcast agreement with Bell Media, DAZN and YouTube for reported $500 million
items.summary_auto: The CFL announced Thursday it has signed a six-year extension with Bell Media to remain its majority broadcaster. The deal also incorporates DAZN as a new Canadian and international broadcast partner along with YouTube being named a Premier Platform partner.

items.title: Potential WW II explosive found near N.L. community, residents offered help evacuating
items.summary_auto: The RCMP is helping Stephenville, N.L. residents who want to evacuate after a suspected military shell was discovered on a nearby St. George's beach and turned over to police on Wednesday.

items.title: Residents offered help evacuating as potential WW II explosive found near Stephenville, N.L.
items.summary_auto: The RCMP is helping Stephenville, N.L. residents who want to evacuate after a suspected military shell was discovered on a nearby St. George's beach and turned over to police on Wednesday.

items.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension to be considered by Trump, American officials say
items.summary_auto: The United States and Iran have reached an outline agreement to extend their ceasefire pending the approval of President Donald Trump, Axios reported on Thursday, after Iran ‌targeted a U.S. airbase in Kuwait in the wake of U.S. strikes on what Washington said was an Iranian drone operation.

items.title: Trump and the politics of corruption
items.summary_auto: Donald Trump's personal net worth has reportedly increased by $3-billion dollars since the start of his second term. Today, we discuss Trump’s myriad corruption allegations, and the politics of self enrichment.

items.title: More than a dozen students killed in overnight fire at girls' school in Kenya, official says
items.summary_auto: A fire tore through a dormitory at a girls' school in a town in Kenya's Rift Valley overnight, killing at least 15 students, police said on Thursday.

items.title: His gut produces alcohol. It took years to get the rare illness diagnosed
items.summary_auto: For nearly 10 years, Eric Poulin has been having episodes where he seems drunk — without taking even a sip of alcohol.

items.title: N.S. couple say they’re on verge of bankruptcy after incomplete renovation
items.summary_auto: A Colchester County couple living in an incomplete renovation say they are on the verge of bankruptcy, while other clients of the same contractor are also crying foul.

items.title: Criminal cases lost due to missed trial deadlines 'not acceptable,' says N.L. premier
items.summary_auto: Newfoundland and Labrador Premier Tony Wakeham says it's "clearly unacceptable" for criminal cases to be tossed from the court system because of trial delays. The province's justice minister now says her department will start to track them, in the wake of a CBC News investigation.

items.title: Celebrities are telling women to use more AI or be 'left behind.' Their followers aren't having it
items.summary_auto: Author and podcast host Mel Robbins received heavy backlash earlier this month for telling her 12.3 million Instagram followers to use more AI, including for sensitive financial information. She's echoing recent messaging from a number of celebrities, including actor Reese Witherspoon, encouraging women to use more AI or risk being "left behind."

items.title: How AI has helped a former reality TV star become a contender for L.A. mayor
items.summary_auto: Some observers say that AI-generated videos have made former reality TV show villain Spencer Pratt a serious contender in the. Los Angeles mayoral race. But others suggest the use of AI in these types of videos could also mark a new era in political campaigns.

items.title: Canada mulls legal action against Colombia over cancelled contract with national defence ministry
items.summary_auto: A federal Crown corporation is mulling legal action against Colombia after the country’s Ministry of National Defence cancelled a contract worth hundreds of millions of dollars to construct new ministry headquarters.

items.title: Hamilton refugee says federal health cuts are forcing him to forgo needed dental care
items.summary_auto: A dentist told Sudanese refugee Omer Obiedallah that he'll likely need several procedures to deal with tooth pain. Due to recent federal cuts to refugee healthcare, Obiedallah says he won't be able to afford to follow up. Instead, he says, he will have to wait till his situation worsens and go to an ER.

items.title: It's crunch time for Canada's trade deal with the U.S. and Mexico
items.summary_auto: Sluggish trade negotiations between Canada and the U.S. are finally showing signs of life as a milestone looms for renewal of CUSMA, their three-way trade deal with Mexico.

items.title: Germany pledges 4 submarines by 2036 in high-stakes pitch to Canada
items.summary_auto: Germany’s defence minister says submarine maker ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems can deliver four Type 212-CD submarines to Canada by 2036, matching a key South Korean promise by reallocating boats from German and Norwegian orders. The bid also includes billions in proposed industrial investments, turning Ottawa’s looming submarine choice into a broader strategic and economic decision.

items.title: Edmonton hospital using medical technology to offer non-surgical weight loss procedure
items.summary_auto: A procedure at the Royal Alexandra Hospital sees tiny tools used to stitch up a stomach to reduce its size.

items.title: Trump administration tells prosecutors to avoid criminal probes of acting Venezuelan president
items.summary_auto: The Trump administration has quietly instructed federal prosecutors in Miami to avoid pursuing criminal investigations into Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez, a longtime target of the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration, according to current and former U.S. law enforcement officials — the latest sign of warming relations between the White House and  the oil-rich nation.

items.title: Report recommends warning labels on booze as B.C. alcohol use above national levels
items.summary_auto: As of 2023, people in B.C. consume an average of 8.8 drinks per week, compared with the national average of 8.2, a new report says.

items.title: Pilot involved in fatal B.C. helicopter accident distracted by cellphone, TSB finds
items.summary_auto: A Transportation Safety Board of Canada investigation into a fatal B.C. helicopter accident states the pilot missed important steps on a safety checklist and was distracted by his cellphone.
